methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Deep Fission, a nuclear energy company founded by Liz and Rich Muller, is being valued following $110M in total capital raised. The valuation universe comprises 11 peers across the nuclear energy sector: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Oklo, Nano Nuclear Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

A peer-median approach was not applicable due to insufficient clustering within narrow filters. While 8 same-sector peers exist, only 2 companies matched the same development stage, with zero peers identified in adjacent stages or similar valuation tiers. This fragmentation—typical of frontier nuclear markets where companies span diverse technology approaches and maturation levels—means a filtered peer median would lack the three-datapoint minimum required for statistical reliability. Broadening to all-sector medians risked introducing category noise given the heterogeneity across reactor designs and commercialization timelines.

The analysis therefore identifies the relevant comp universe but cannot support a point valuation or range through triangulation at this time. Resolution would require either: (1) identifying additional same-stage or adjacent-stage nuclear peers meeting valuation-tier criteria; (2) gathering more granular financial metrics (revenue, burn rate, capex requirements) from the 11 identified peers to enable comparable analysis; or (3) deploying scenario-based methods tied to specific milestones (licensing, first-of-a-kind deployment). The $110M raised provides a floor reference but does not alone establish fair value absent comparable transaction or operating data.
